_comment: Process → app attribution for the Machines-tab diagnostics capture (issue #315).
Raw process names are useless on this fleet ('python.exe' x14); these rules map a
process to the app that owns it, so a capture reports 'app-launcher: 3 procs / 800 MB'.
Edit this file to teach the sampler a new app — it is data, not code.
Matching order: fleet_roots (a venv/checkout path wins, most specific first) →
binaries (known executable names) → cmdline_contains → path_prefixes (the broad
net: an OS-owned directory identifies processes whose names mean nothing alone) →
fallback 'unattributed', which is exactly the review list of things nobody has
All matching is case-insensitive and normalizes \ to / before comparing.
Any group may carry a _windows / _darwin / _linux twin, merged in only on that
platform. This is not cosmetic: /usr/bin is Apple-owned and SIP-protected on
macOS but is where ordinary user software lives on Linux, so bucketing it as
'system' is right on one and actively wrong on the other (#320).
Deliberate boundary: only OS-owned paths are bucketed. /Applications and
/opt/homebrew are NOT, because user-installed software is precisely the bloat
this capture exists to surface — auto-bucketing it would hide the answer.
_fleet_roots_darwin_comment: Intentionally empty. mac-mini-m4 checks the hub out at /Users/roberto/local-llm-hub
— directly in the home dir, alongside 10 unrelated folders — so adding the home dir
as a fleet root would mint a bogus app id for every one of them to attribute a
single repo. The Mac's hub is caught by the '-m src.server' cmdline rule instead.
Revisit if the Mac ever grows a real ~/automation tree.
_binaries_windows_comment: Windows-only names that collide with a real, different process on Unix.
'ControlCenter' was the live bug: Elgato's Control Center on Windows shares a
name with Apple's, so every macOS capture reported the OS shell as 'elgato'.
'bash' and 'dwm' (a Linux window manager) are the same class of collision;
'system' is a Windows concept. Merged on Windows only, so Windows is unchanged.
_path_prefixes_comment: The broad net, matched last and longest-prefix-first. On macOS this is what
does the real work: 310 of 673 processes are root/_service daemons whose
command line the hub cannot read, leaving only a 16-char truncated kernel name
('AppleCredentialM'). Their executable path IS readable, and these prefixes
turned 667 of 673 into a named bucket. Only SIP/OS-owned roots appear here.
_path_prefixes_linux_comment: Deliberately excludes /usr/bin and /bin: unlike macOS, that is where ordinary
user software lives on Linux, and bucketing it as 'system' would hide the very
processes a capture is meant to surface. Unverified against a live Linux
capture — openclaw runs no hub yet (see #316).
